demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; usually walks with a four-wheeled walker

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I feel like I am drowning every time I try to lie down to sleep, and my legs are tight as drums.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Her breathing has got much worse over the last few days and last night she woke up gasping and panicking. She can't lie flat now and her legs are more and more swollen. (Reported partly by her husband, who is with her.)

symptom_narrative.onset: gradually over the past five days, getting much worse in the last two days
symptom_narrative.duration: about five days, sharply worse over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: breathless and worn out on the smallest effort; a heavy feeling in the chest and a nagging cough; wakes at night gasping for air
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst her breathing has ever been; frightening, like she is suffocating when she lies down
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest and legs — heaviness in the chest, tight swollen legs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse when lying flat and at night; a bit easier sitting propped up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting upright, propped on three pillows
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying flat, and any walking or effort
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: swollen tight legs, a persistent cough, exhaustion; she says there is no fever and denies any sharp or crushing chest pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: has a long-standing heart problem she takes a fluid (water) tablet for, but says it has never been this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: was getting around the house with her walker; now stuck in the armchair because walking leaves her too breathless; frightened of falling if she rushes to the toilet at night

past_medical_history.how_managed: takes a daily fluid (water) tablet for it

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: fluid tablet (water tablet) for her heart

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her husband, who is exhausted from being up with her at night and made the call; usually mobilises with a four-wheeled walker; the bedroom floor is cluttered which worries them both given her unsteadiness

communication_style.typical_message_style: short, frightened messages, often relayed through her husband

access_barriers.transport: does not drive; relies on her elderly husband; getting out at night is difficult

objective_data_offered.value: 93% on the fingertip clip we keep at home
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a cheap home fingertip oximeter, cold hands, may be unreliable

objective_data_offered.value: about 150 over 90 on the home arm cuff
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home cuff

objective_data_offered.value: around 95 to 100 beats per minute on the same home cuff
84 kg on the bathroom scales — up a few kilos on her usual
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home bathroom scales; no consistent recent baseline recorded

objective_data_offered.value: sitting bolt upright in an armchair, breathing fast and using her neck and shoulder muscles to breathe, looks tired and distressed; visible swelling of both lower legs

husband reports she cannot lie flat, has been sleeping propped on three pillows, woke last night gasping and panicking, and both her legs are swollen and tight up to the shins; he says she feels no hotter than usual and has had no fever
